zh.of Grappenhall, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾格拉本哈爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Caldwall於大約1612年出生在英國爾格拉本哈爾時，他的父親George Caldwell32歲，母親Elizabeth Fleetwood29歲。他於大約1636年與Ceicly在英國英格蘭爾格拉本哈爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.of Grappenhall, Cheshire, England: 英国英格兰爾格拉本哈爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Caldwall于大约1612年出生在英国爾格拉本哈爾时，他的父亲George Caldwell32岁，母亲Elizabeth Fleetwood29岁。他于大约1636年与Ceicly在英国英格兰爾格拉本哈爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Caldwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places in England and Scotland, variously spelled, that are named with Old English ceald ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Caldwell in North Yorkshire is one major source of the surname; Caldwell in Renfrewshire in Scotland another. Possibly also from Caldwell (Warwickshire), Caldwall (Worcestershire), Cauldwell (Bedfordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire), Caudle Green (Gloucestershire), Caudle Ditch or Cawdle Fen (Cambridgeshire), Chadwell (Essex, Hertfordshire, Leicestershire, Wiltshire), Chardwell (Essex), or Chardle Ditch (Cambridgeshire, early recorded as Kadewelle).

Irish: when not the English surname, this is an Anglicized form of Ó Fuarghuis or Ó hUarghusa ‘descendant of (F)uarghus’, a personal name whose literal sense ‘cold’ + ‘choice’ was reinterpreted as coming from fuaruisce ‘cold water’.

History: Several Caldwells emigrated from Scotland to America by way of Ireland in the 18th century. James Caldwell (1734–81), a son of settler John Caldwell, was born in Charlotte County, VA, and was a militant clergyman during the revolutionary war. Andrew Caldwell, a Scottish farmer, emigrated to North America in 1718 and started a family in Lancaster County, PA. His son David was a Presbyterian clergyman and well-known revolutionary war patriot.

variants.name: Cadle
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Irish, both of Norman origin: nickname from Old French cadel ‘leader’.
English: variant of   Caldwell   and of   Caudill  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Kadel  .

variants.name: Cardwell
meanings: English and Scottish: in northern England, the West Midlands, and southwest Scotland, this surname is a variant of Caldwell . In southwestern England it is probably a variant of Cardall, a habitational name from Cardwell in Milton Abbot (Devon). See also Cardell .

variants.name: Cadwell
meanings: (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one of two Devon places, either Cadewell (House) in Cockington or Caddywell in Great Torrington.

variants.name: Caudill
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English caudel, cadel, caudell(e) ‘hot fortified drink, esp. one containing spiced wine or ale’, also a thin gruel (Anglo-Norman French caudel, medieval Latin caldellum). Alternatively, a variant of Caldwell . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cordwell
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Caldwell . Alternatively, in Derbyshire a habitational name from Cordwell in Holmsfield, Derbyshire, named with Old English cauld ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Caldwall was born about 1612, in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, his father, George Caldwell, was 32 and his mother, Elizabeth Fleetwood, was 29. He married Ceicly about 1636, in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Caldwall est né environ 1612, à Grappenhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, George Caldwell, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Fleetwood, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Ceicly environ 1636, à Grappenhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Caldwall um 1612 in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater George Caldwell 32 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Fleetwood 29. Er hat um 1636 in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Ceicly geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Caldwall è nato il circa 1612 nel Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, George Caldwell, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Fleetwood, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Ceicly circa 1636, nel Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Caldwall(이)가 대략 1612년 영국 체실르 그랩홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Caldwell(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Fleetwood(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1636년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 그랩홀에서 Ceicly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

Ele casou-se com Ceicly aproximadamente 1636, em Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Caldwall родился около 1612 г. в местности граппенолл, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени George Caldwell было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Fleetwood, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Ceicly около 1636 г. в местности граппенолл, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Caldwall nació aproximadamente 1612, en Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, George Caldwell, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Fleetwood, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Ceicly aproximadamente 1636, en Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas.
